heading: Review of a sentence

(e). , in concluding provisions, substituted “, except with respect to determinations under subsection (3)(A) or (3)(B), shall give due deference to the district court’s application of the guidelines to the facts. children.text: was imposed in violation of law;
was imposed as a result of an incorrect application of the sentencing guidelines; or

is greater than the sentence specified in the applicable guideline range to the extent that the sentence includes a greater fine or term of imprisonment, probation, or supervised release than the maximum established in the guideline range, or includes a more limiting condition of probation or supervised release under section 3563(b)(6) or (b)(11) than the maximum established in the guideline range; or1See References in Text note below.1
was imposed for an offense for which there is no sentencing guideline and is plainly unreasonable.
was imposed in violation of law;
was imposed as a result of an incorrect application of the sentencing guidelines;

is less than the sentence specified in the applicable guideline range to the extent that the sentence includes a lesser fine or term of imprisonment, probation, or supervised release than the minimum established in the guideline range, or includes a less limiting condition of probation or supervised release under section 3563(b)(6) or (b)(11) than the minimum established in the guideline range; or1
was imposed for an offense for which there is no sentencing guideline and is plainly unreasonable.
a defendant may not file a notice of appeal under paragraph (3) or (4) of subsection (a) unless the sentence imposed is greater than the sentence set forth in such agreement; and
the Government may not file a notice of appeal under paragraph (3) or (4) of subsection (b) unless the sentence imposed is less than the sentence set forth in such agreement.
that portion of the record in the case that is designated as pertinent by either of the parties;
the presentence report; and
the information submitted during the sentencing proceeding.
was imposed in violation of law;
was imposed as a result of an incorrect application of the sentencing guidelines;

children.label: A
children.text: the district court failed to provide the written statement of reasons required by section 3553(c);

children.label: i
children.text: does not advance the objectives set forth in section 3553(a)(2); or

children.label: ii
children.text: is not authorized under section 3553(b); or

children.label: iii
children.text: is not justified by the facts of the case; or

children.label: C
children.text: the sentence departs to an unreasonable degree from the applicable guidelines range, having regard for the factors to be considered in imposing a sentence, as set forth in and the reasons for the imposition of the particular sentence, as stated by the district court pursuant to the provisions of section 3553(c); orsection 3553(a) of this title
was imposed for an offense for which there is no applicable sentencing guideline and is plainly unreasonable.

the sentence was imposed in violation of law or imposed as a result of an incorrect application of the sentencing guidelines, the court shall remand the case for further sentencing proceedings with such instructions as the court considers appropriate;

children.label: A
children.text: if it determines that the sentence is too high and the appeal has been filed under subsection (a), it shall set aside the sentence and remand the case for further sentencing proceedings with such instructions as the court considers appropriate, subject to subsection (g);

children.label: B
children.text: if it determines that the sentence is too low and the appeal has been filed under subsection (b), it shall set aside the sentence and remand the case for further sentencing proceedings with such instructions as the court considers appropriate, subject to subsection (g);
the sentence is not described in paragraph (1) or (2), it shall affirm the sentence.

In determining the range referred to in subsection 3553(a)(4), the court shall apply the guidelines issued by the Sentencing Commission pursuant to , United States Code, and that were in effect on the date of the previous sentencing of the defendant prior to the appeal, together with any amendments thereto by any act of Congress that was in effect on such date; andsection 994(a)(1) of title 28

children.label: A
children.text: was specifically and affirmatively included in the written statement of reasons required by section 3553(c) in connection with the previous sentencing of the defendant prior to the appeal; and

children.label: B
children.text: was held by the court of appeals, in remanding the case, to be a permissible ground of departure.

children.heading: .—Application to a Sentence by a Magistrate Judge
children.label: h .—Application to a Sentence by a Magistrate Judge
children.text: An appeal of an otherwise final sentence imposed by a United States magistrate judge may be taken to a judge of the district court, and this section shall apply (except for the requirement of approval by the Attorney General or the Solicitor General in the case of a Government appeal) as though the appeal were to a court of appeals from a sentence imposed by a district court.

children.heading: .—Guideline Not Expressed as a Range
children.label: i .—Guideline Not Expressed as a Range
children.text: For the purpose of this section, the term “guideline range” includes a guideline range having the same upper and lower limits.

children.label: A
children.text: advances the objectives set forth in section 3553(a)(2); and

children.label: B
children.text: is authorized under section 3553(b); and

children.label: C
children.text: is justified by the facts of the case; and
a factor is an “impermissible” ground of departure if it is not a permissible factor within the meaning of subsection (j)(1).
